steps.title: Seeing Through
steps.text:
An **X-ray attack** is when one of your long-range pieces threatens or defends *through* an intervening piece. The piece in front blocks the immediate attack, but if it ever moves — or is captured — the X-ray crashes through.

## The geometry

Four things on one line:

1. Your **rook**, **bishop**, or **queen** at one end.
2. Your **own piece** (or sometimes the enemy's) standing in the way.
3. The line continues *past* the blocker.
4. A target — usually a king, queen, or critical defender — sits at the far end.

Unlike a pin (which freezes a piece) or a skewer (which chases one off), the X-ray simply *sees past* whatever's in the middle.

## Two uses of the X-ray

- **Offensive X-ray** — your queen X-rays the enemy king through their own piece. Trade the blocker and the attack lands.
- **Defensive X-ray** — your rook X-rays one of your own pieces, defending it through another defender. After exchanges, the X-ray re-establishes guard.

## How to spot it

1. Trace every long-range piece you have, including beyond blockers.
2. Look for **king or queen** sitting on the far end of one of those lines.
3. Plan an exchange that removes the blocker.
4. After the smoke clears, the X-ray is suddenly an open attack.

In this position, White's rooks on d1 and d2 stack with the enemy king's rook on d8 down the d-file. After a series of trades, the *back* rook on d1 X-rays through and lands on the king.

> A pin freezes. A skewer chases. An X-ray *waits* — and wins anyway.

steps.title: Calculating Through Trades
steps.text:
X-rays often decide who comes out ahead in a chain of captures on the same square.

## The procedure

1. Identify the file, rank, or diagonal where pieces are stacked.
2. Count attackers and defenders.
3. Imagine the exchanges happening in order, *cheapest first*.
4. Whoever has the **last attacker** on the line wins the square.

## In this position

White has *two* rooks on the d-file. Black has one. After a sequence like {{Rxd8+}} {{Rxd8}} {{Rxd8+}}, White lands a rook on the eighth rank checking the king — the X-ray is the reason it works.

> X-ray vision is just patient counting along a single line.
